doubtful_osteoarthritis: An X-ray image of a knee with doubtful osteoarthritis, showing very minimal or questionable signs of joint degeneration.
This knee X-ray presents subtle or ambiguous changes that may suggest the earliest stages of osteoarthritis.
There are no definite radiographic features of osteoarthritis, but minor irregularities may be present.
The joint space appears mostly preserved, with only slight or questionable narrowing.
Osteophyte formation, if present, is minimal and not clearly diagnostic.
healthy_knee: An X-ray image of a healthy knee joint with no visible signs of osteoarthritis or abnormality.
This knee X-ray demonstrates normal bone structure and joint space, with no evidence of disease.
The image shows a knee with well-preserved cartilage and no osteophyte formation.
There are no signs of joint space narrowing, sclerosis, or bone deformity in this healthy knee.
A typical example of a normal knee X-ray, free from any pathological findings.
minimal_osteoarthritis: An X-ray image of a knee with minimal osteoarthritis, showing slight joint space narrowing or small osteophytes.
This knee X-ray reveals early signs of osteoarthritis, such as minor bone spur formation.
There may be subtle narrowing of the joint space, but the overall structure remains largely intact.
Minimal osteoarthritis is characterized by faint radiographic changes without significant functional impairment.
The image displays the earliest detectable features of osteoarthritis, including tiny osteophytes.
moderate_osteoarthritis: An X-ray image of a knee with moderate osteoarthritis, showing clear joint space narrowing, moderate osteophytes, and possible sclerosis.
This knee X-ray demonstrates definite loss of cartilage thickness and more prominent bone spurs.
Moderate osteoarthritis is marked by visible changes in the joint, including sclerosis of the bone ends.
The joint space is noticeably reduced, and osteophyte formation is more pronounced.
There may be mild deformity of the joint surfaces due to ongoing degenerative changes.
severe_osteoarthritis: An X-ray image of a knee with severe osteoarthritis, showing marked joint space narrowing, large osteophytes, and significant bone changes.
This knee X-ray reveals advanced degenerative changes, including almost complete loss of joint space.
Severe osteoarthritis is characterized by large bone spurs, subchondral sclerosis, and possible bone deformity.
The image shows extensive cartilage loss and may display bone-on-bone contact.
There is significant joint deformity and structural damage in this advanced stage of osteoarthritis.
